summary: Matched two Mushroom Kingdom grkinokoroute stage callback functions, added needed includes, and replaced two header UNK prototypes with recovered signatures. The PR body claims objdiff 100% verification and clean ninja build; the bot report corroborates 100% matches for grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660.

key_files.role: Implements grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660, adds includes for camera/effect/material/JObj-dependent calls, and uses PAD_STACK in both matched functions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary lists grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660

key_files.role: Updates recovered prototypes for the two newly matched functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grKinokoRoute_802084B4 prototype changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void grKinokoRoute_802084B4(HSD_GObj*)
raw_diff: grKinokoRoute_80208660 prototype changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool grKinokoRoute_80208660(s32, HSD_GObj*)

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder for grKinokoRoute_802084B4 with a void callback taking HSD_GObj*, reading a padded ground/user-data struct to reach an HSD_JObj*, setting JObj flags, toggling a flag on another ground object if present, spawning effect 0x442 at the JObj position, updating camera/ground state, applying grMaterial_801C8CDC, and using PAD_STACK(20).

Replaced the placeholder for grKinokoRoute_80208660 with a bool callback taking s32 and HSD_GObj*, reading fighter position and velocity through ftLib helpers, checking y-position/velocity thresholds against 5.5F, spawning/triggering grKinokoRoute_802086EC with a scale from ftLib_80086B80(gobj) / 10.0F, calling Ground_801C53EC(0x77A10), using PAD_STACK(8), and returning false.
Added includes for ef/efsync.h, gr/grmaterial.h, and baselib/jobj.h to support the recovered code.
Updated grkinokoroute.h prototypes for grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660 from unknown signatures to recovered typed signatures.
smart_moves: Header prototypes were updated alongside recovered function bodies instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS after signatures became apparent.

The code uses existing subsystem helpers such as HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, lb_8000B1CC, efSync_Spawn, Camera_80030E44, ftLib_80086644, ftLib_80086684, ftLib_80086B80, and ground/material helpers rather than inventing local stand-ins.
Null checks were preserved before writing through the secondary ground object's user_data pointer.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched body proves a stage callback signature, update the owning header prototype in the same PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grkinokoroute.h prototype updates for grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stage callback matches, existing stage, ground, effect, camera, material, and fighter-library helpers can express side effects cleanly while preserving typed call surfaces.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftLib helper calls and grKinokoRoute_802086EC call in grKinokoRoute_80208660

naming_conventions.name: gp
naming_conventions.context: Local pointer assigned from gobj->user_data in grKinokoRoute_802084B4, matching common short local role naming for ground/user-data pointers.
evidence_refs: decomp_standards: conservative-naming permits short local role names when roles are clear

naming_conventions.name: sp_vec, pos, vel, scale
naming_conventions.context: Locals use role-oriented names for vector/effect position, fighter position, velocity, and scale calculation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Vec3 sp_vec; Vec3 pos; Vec3 vel; f32 scale

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used narrow PAD_STACK calls to preserve stack shape in both newly matched functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: grKinokoRoute_802084B4 ends with PAD_STACK(20); grKinokoRoute_80208660 uses PAD_STACK(8). PR body claims objdiff 100% and bot report lists both functions at 100%, which is enough intake evidence to record this as a PR-specific tactic, not a global style recommendation.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: All functions verified 100% match via objdiff during overnight run
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660 as 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a temporary padded struct to access a known HSD_JObj* field at offset 0xDD8 from gobj->user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The padded struct expresses the JObj field used by HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll and lb_8000B1CC, but the PR does not provide type-recovery evidence beyond the matching diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct { char pad[0xDD8]; HSD_JObj* jobj; }* gp = gobj->user_data;

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used raw offset access through UnkFlagStruct at user_data + 0xC4 for a secondary ground object flag write.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: This is evidence of a match tactic or incomplete type recovery; current standards would prefer a real field when recoverable, so this should not be promoted as a preferred convention.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported matched code +2644 bytes overall and specifically listed grKinokoRoute_802084B4 (+176 bytes) and grKinokoRoute_80208660 (+140 bytes) as 100.00% after this PR head.
No review comments or review records are present in the loaded PR slice.

evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report for GALE01 lists both target functions at 100.00%
notes: Changed files and code edits are strongly evidenced by the loaded diff and changed_files list.
Verification is supported by the PR author's claim and the bot report for the two target functions, but no raw objdiff logs or local build logs are included.
The bot report includes many unrelated item matches and .sdata2 improvements outside the two changed files; this intake does not attribute those to this PR beyond noting the report content.
No human review feedback is available in the loaded slice.
The PR body states the functions handle moving platforms and hazard spawning routes, but deeper gameplay semantics are not independently proven by comments or source names in the diff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered grkinokoroute signatures should update the owning header
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2302 matched grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660 and updated src/melee/gr/grkinokoroute.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void grKinokoRoute_802084B4(HSD_GObj*) and bool grKinokoRoute_80208660(s32, HSD_GObj*). This is a concrete example of truthful header cleanup accompanying matched bodies.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary lists the two matched functions

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR-specific PAD_STACK use in grkinokoroute matched callbacks
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2302 used PAD_STACK(20) in grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and PAD_STACK(8) in grKinokoRoute_80208660. The PR body claims 100% objdiff verification and the bot report lists both functions as 100.00%, so this is useful as a searchable PR-specific tactic, not a general recommendation to add PAD_STACK without evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the temporary padded user_data struct at offset 0xDD8 as a path fact or final type layout without corroborating type/source evidence.
Do not promote the user_data + 0xC4 UnkFlagStruct write as preferred style; it remains pointer-math/partial-type evidence and may be a future type-recovery target.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale because the loaded slice contains no human review comments.
Do not claim that the unrelated item matches in the bot report were caused by the grkinokoroute source changes.
